# Test Quality Review — ai/helpers

**Scope:** `lib/features/ai/helpers/` (impl) + `test/features/ai/helpers/` (tests)
**Generated by automated test-quality review (static analysis)**

---

## File size / split opportunities

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/helpers/prompt_builder_helper_test.dart` — **2 023 lines** (more than 4× the ~500-line guideline). The single source file `prompt_builder_helper.dart` already has **four** test files against it, violating the "one test file per source file" rule from `AGENTS.md`. The four files (`prompt_builder_helper_test.dart`, `prompt_builder_helper_speech_dictionary_test.dart`, `prompt_builder_helper_audio_transcript_test.dart`, `prompt_builder_helper_current_task_summary_test.dart`) should be **merged back** into a single canonical file, then trimmed with shared helpers. Until then, the split itself is the primary violation to resolve.

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/helpers/prompt_capability_filter_test.dart` — **1 163 lines** for a 105-line impl. The `isPromptAvailableOnPlatform` desktop group at lines 162–296 contains five nearly-identical tests that each create a prompt+model+provider triple and stub `getConfigById`. These should be parameterized via a loop or a table-driven helper `_stubAndCheck(...)`.

- [ ] **[MED]** `lib/features/ai/helpers/prompt_builder_helper.dart` — **629 lines**, above the preferred 500-line mark. Natural split seams: lines 61–232 (`buildPromptWithData` and all placeholder branches) vs lines 389–628 (private helpers `_getTaskJsonForEntity`, `_findLinkedTask`, `_getLanguageCodeForEntity`, `getSpeechDictionaryTerms`, `_buildSpeechDictionaryPromptText`, `_buildCorrectionExamplesPromptText`, `_resolveEntryText`, `_resolveAudioTranscript`). The private helpers could be extracted into a `PromptPlaceholderResolver` or similar internal class.

---

## Test quality improvements

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/helpers/prompt_builder_helper_speech_dictionary_test.dart:101–104, 128–130, 582–585` — uses raw `getIt.isRegistered<EntitiesCacheService>()` / `getIt.unregister<EntitiesCacheService>()` / `getIt.registerSingleton<EntitiesCacheService>(...)` boilerplate in `setUp`/`tearDown` and inline. `AGENTS.md` mandates `setUpTestGetIt()` / `tearDownTestGetIt()` with `additionalSetup:` for this purpose. Same pattern repeated in `prompt_builder_helper_audio_transcript_test.dart:145–148, 172–173` and `prompt_builder_helper_current_task_summary_test.dart:135–138, 162–163`.

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/helpers/automatic_image_analysis_trigger_test.dart:43–46, 83–85` — raw `getIt.isRegistered<DomainLogger>()` / `getIt.unregister<DomainLogger>()` / `getIt.registerSingleton<DomainLogger>(...)` in `setUp`/`tearDown` instead of `setUpTestGetIt()` + `additionalSetup`.

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/helpers/prompt_builder_helper_test.dart` — every test case re-declares a full `AiConfigPrompt` constructor (11–14 lines each) with only `userMessage`, `aiResponseType`, and `requiredInputData` varying. There are at least 20 such blocks. A file-level factory `_makePromptConfig({String userMessage = '...', AiResponseType responseType = ..., List<InputDataType> inputs = const []})` would reduce each call to 1–2 lines and cut the file by ~300 lines.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/helpers/prompt_capability_filter_test.dart:162–296` — five desktop tests at lines 162–296 all test the same property ("desktop returns true regardless of config"). This is a copy-paste permutation (null model, wrong model type, null provider, wrong provider type, etc.). A single parameterized loop over the scenarios would cover the same ground in 20 lines.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/helpers/prompt_capability_filter_mobile_test.dart` — every test body opens with `if (Platform.isMacOS || Platform.isLinux || Platform.isWindows) { return; }` (702 lines, ~15 tests). This pattern silently passes on desktop. Use `@TestOn('!windows && !mac-os && !linux')` at the file level (or `skip:` in `group()`), or restructure to use Riverpod mocking of `isDesktop`.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/helpers/entity_state_helper_test.dart` — five individual `test()` cases for `getCurrentEntityState` each fully construct a `JournalImage` fixture (30-line blocks). These differ only in mock behavior. A `_runCase({bool entityFound, bool typeMatches, bool throws})` helper parameterized over the three axes would DRY this to a single table.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/features/ai/helpers/prompt_builder_helper_test.dart:1891–2021` — the `_logPlaceholderFailure on placeholder build error` group re-defines `correctionExamplesConfig()` and `audioEntity(...)` helpers that are identical to the same helpers defined in the `correction_examples placeholder injection` group (lines 1349–1360, 1912–1927). Extract these to file level.

---

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/features/ai/helpers/skill_prompt_builder.dart` — `SkillPromptBuilder.build` is a pure, deterministic function that assembles text from a discrete set of enum inputs (`SkillType`, `ContextPolicy`) plus optional strings. Current tests (342 lines) use 18 hand-rolled `test()` cases, each exercising one flag combination at a time. A Glados property over `(SkillType × ContextPolicy × optional-string-presence)` can exhaustively verify structural invariants: e.g. "speaker rules appear iff `skillType == transcription`", "URL rules appear iff `skillType == imageAnalysis`", "task context appears iff `contextPolicy == fullTask || taskSummary`". This is a textbook Glados fit — pure function, combinatorial input space, algebraic invariants. No I/O involved.

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `lib/features/ai/helpers/prompt_builder_helper.dart` (`getEffectiveMessage` / `_buildSpeechDictionaryPromptText` / `_buildCorrectionExamplesPromptText`) — the `escapeForJson` lambda inside `_buildSpeechDictionaryPromptText` (line 467–471) and the correction-examples `escapedBefore`/`escapedAfter` escaping (lines 561–562) are pure string transforms with no I/O. A Glados round-trip property — generate arbitrary strings containing `"`, `\`, `\n`, and verify that the resulting injected text is well-formed — is valuable and cannot be covered by hand.

- [ ] **[MED]** `lib/features/ai/helpers/prompt_capability_filter.dart` (`isLocalOnlyProviderType`) — pure function over a small closed enum (`InferenceProviderType`). A property `∀ t: isLocalOnlyProviderType(t) == (t ∈ {whisper, ollama, voxtral, mlxAudio})` would auto-detect any future enum variant that is incorrectly classified. `profile_locality_test.dart` has similar coverage but exercising it through the full stack; a unit Glados test on the static predicate would run faster and shrink more cleanly.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `lib/features/ai/helpers/profile_locality.dart` (`profileIsLocal`) — already well-tested with hand-written cases but no property tests. A Glados scenario generator over `(Set<providerModelId> × InferenceProviderType per slot)` could enforce the "fail-closed on missing model/provider" invariant across any combination of slot counts and provider types.

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/helpers/prompt_builder_helper_test.dart` — the `buildSystemMessageWithData` method (lines 87–95 of impl) is not directly tested. It currently just delegates to `getEffectiveMessage` and always ignores the entity, which is correct but undocumented. At minimum, one test should confirm the method ignores `{{speech_dictionary}}` and `{{task}}` placeholders (per the doc-comment "Note: `{{speech_dictionary}}` is only supported in user messages").

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/helpers/prompt_builder_helper_test.dart` — the `_findLinkedTask` fallback direction (lines 372–379 of impl: `getLinkedToEntities` when `getLinkedEntities` returns no task) is exercised in `prompt_builder_helper_speech_dictionary_test.dart` (line 582) but not in the main test file. This is an indirect consequence of the multi-file split — coverage of this path is scattered and hard to find.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/helpers/skill_prompt_builder_test.dart` — `SkillType.imagePromptGeneration` with `contextPolicy == fullTask` is tested for `entryContent` (line 296) but **not** for the task context injection path (`_appendTaskContext` → full JSON branch). The `imagePromptGeneration` skill type falls through to the "other skills" branch in `_appendTaskContext`, so that code path has no direct test.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/helpers/profile_locality_test.dart` — the deduplication logic ("same model id in multiple slots is only looked up once") is mentioned in the impl comment but there is no test verifying that a profile reusing the same `providerModelId` across both `thinkingModelId` and `thinkingHighEndModelId` still resolves to one repository lookup rather than two.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/features/ai/helpers/entity_state_helper_test.dart` — tests cover `JournalImage` type mismatches but not the case where the entity is a `JournalAudio` (a different concrete type). The generic `<T>` mechanism is therefore tested for only one of the two realistic entity types.

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/helpers/prompt_builder_helper_speech_dictionary_test.dart` — raw `setUp`/`tearDown` that calls `getIt.isRegistered` + `getIt.unregister` + `getIt.registerSingleton` on every test re-registers a `MockEntitiesCacheService` from scratch. Using `setUpTestGetIt()` (which uses a shared `getIt` scope) would avoid repeated registration overhead across ~20 tests in this file.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/helpers/prompt_builder_helper_audio_transcript_test.dart` and `prompt_builder_helper_current_task_summary_test.dart` — same raw GetIt per-test cost as above. Both files also duplicate the `testTask` and `testAudio` fixture definitions that are already present in `prompt_builder_helper_speech_dictionary_test.dart`. Merging into one file (per the one-test-file-per-source-file rule) and sharing a file-level setup would remove 3× repeated fixture initialization.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/features/ai/helpers/prompt_capability_filter_mobile_test.dart` — 702 lines of tests that silently return early on desktop (the current CI and dev machine target). These tests contribute no coverage on the build environment. Until a mobile CI target exists, tagging them with `@TestOn('android || ios')` would exclude them from the desktop shard entirely, saving their trivial-but-nonzero warm-up cost.

---

## Summary

- **1 oversized impl file**: `prompt_builder_helper.dart` (629 L; split seam at placeholder-logic vs helper-logic boundary).
- **2 oversized test files**: `prompt_builder_helper_test.dart` (2 023 L) and `prompt_capability_filter_test.dart` (1 163 L); both have significant copy-paste-permutation debt.
- **4 test files** violate one-test-file-per-source-file rule against `prompt_builder_helper.dart` — merging is higher value than further splitting.
- **3+ test files** use raw `getIt.isRegistered`/`unregister`/`registerSingleton` instead of `setUpTestGetIt()` as mandated by `AGENTS.md`.
- **2 strong Glados candidates**: `SkillPromptBuilder.build` (pure function, combinatorial enum flags, rich invariants) and the JSON-escape logic in `_buildSpeechDictionaryPromptText`/`_buildCorrectionExamplesPromptText`.
- **Biggest opportunity**: Merge the four `prompt_builder_helper_*_test.dart` files into one, introduce a `_makePromptConfig(...)` factory, and add Glados coverage for `SkillPromptBuilder` — three changes that would resolve the size violation, the DRY violation, and the generative gap simultaneously.
